item.title: Oil prices surge as Trump suggests U.S. fees on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump's declaration that the U.S. will control the Strait of Hormuz and charge fees on cargo has sparked a second day of oil price rises.

item.title: U.S. uses sea drones in combat for first time to attack Iranian port
item.description: The U.S. military shared video of what it said was its first use of sea drones in combat, to attack an Iranian submarine and ship maintenance facility.

item.title: New York temporarily bans construction on new data centers
item.description: There will be a one-year pause on building new data centers across New York to allow state officials to establish guidelines protecting residents and the environment.

item.title: Military action against Iran formally restarted last week, Trump told lawmakers
item.description: President Trump formally notified Congress that "military action" against Iran restarted last week in a letter obtained by CBS News, as a monthslong ceasefire comes to an end.

item.title: Astronomer's name deciphered from ancient Mayan ruins for first time
item.description: The name "White-chested Fox" was found in drawings dating from 400 BC to 900 AD at the San Bartolo-Xultun archaeological site.

item.title: Why gaining full control of Strait of Hormuz has proven so hard for U.S.
item.description: Restoring oil tanker traffic in the vital Middle East shipping corridor to prewar levels likely will require a much bigger armada of U.S. warships if not tens of thousands of American troops on Iranian soil, experts say.

item.title: They Left NYC for a $13,000 House in Italy | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description: Cassandra and her husband made headlines after leaving New York City to buy a home in rural Italy for just $13,000. She breaks down what motivated the move, how she's adjusted to life in a tiny mountain town, embraced a lower-cost lifestyle and whether trading the conventional American dream for a simpler life abroad was worth it. Plus, Jill and Mark explain what SpaceX's inclusion in Nasdaq index funds actually means for investors, and why one TikToker's recommended tax loophole is complete nonsense.

item.title: Climbers found dead in Italian Alps week after going missing
item.description: The two climbers had not made contact since leaving a mountain refuge on July 9, according to authorities.

item.title: States call out big businesses with employees on Medicaid
item.description: With the federal Medicaid work requirement looming in January, Democrats are considering state legislation to call out big companies that employ workers enrolled in the safety net health program.

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Maine was not intended target of warrant, lawmakers say
item.description: Maine Sen. Angus King said he told Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that he wanted a transparent investigation into the shooting in Biddeford.

item.title: Deputy U.S. marshal shot dead serving arrest warrant in Louisiana, agency says
item.description: A deputy U.S. marshal was shot and killed while serving an arrest warrant on a fugitive in Louisiana, authorities say. The suspect is in custody.

item.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate after his sudden death?
item.description: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death late Saturday has set off a scramble for who will succeed him in the Senate.

item.title: Music group "still shocked" after bandmates killed in small plane crash
item.description: Surviving members of the beloved Bahamian musical group Da Pond Band are speaking out about their friends who were killed when a small plane crashed in the Bahamas on Friday, killing 10 people.

item.title: Surviving band members speak after deadly Bahamas plane crash
item.description: Surviving members of the beloved Bahamian musical group Da Pond Band are speaking out about their friends and bandmates who were killed when a small plane operated by Flamingo Air crashed in the Bahamas on Friday, killing all 10 people on board.

item.title: Judge rebukes Trump and DOJ over IRS lawsuit in scathing decision
item.description: U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams said President Trump's lawsuit against the IRS had been filed for an "improper purpose."

item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: The suit poses a new challenge to the $110 billion deal that would unite two of the nation's largest media companies.

item.title: Judge rebukes Trump and DOJ over IRS lawsuit, refers lawyer for disciplinary proceedings
item.description: A federal judge on Monday imposed professional penalties against lawyers representing President Trump in a civil lawsuit he brought against the IRS and harshly criticized the Justice Department for its handling of the case, concluding that the suit was brought for an "improper purpose." CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: 12 states sue to block merger between Paramount, Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: A coalition of a dozen states on Monday sued to block Paramount Skydance's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, posing a new challenge to the $110 billion deal that would unite two of the nation's largest media companies. Paramount Skydance is the parent company of CBS News. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Summer music highlights of 2026
item.description: Summer is the time to enjoy live music, indoors and out. Scroll through our gallery of some of 2026's leading musical acts, featuring images by CBS News photojournalist Jake Barlow and photographers Ed Spinelli and Kirstine Walton.

item.title: Maps show heat alerts as extreme temperatures threaten millions
item.description: Heat alerts were issued for millions across parts of the western U.S. Sunday due to an unusually prolonged heat dome, which is starting to move east.

item.title: Republican strategist on what he'll remember most about Lindsey Graham
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham is being remembered for his wit and political effectiveness after his sudden death over the weekend. Republican strategist Matt Whitlock joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Third straight night of U.S. strikes against Iran
item.description: CENTCOM says the U.S. is carrying out a third consecutive night of strikes against Iran. CBS News' Kathryn Watson reports.

item.title: Latest on Lindsey Graham's death as tributes pour in and sister picked to fill Senate seat
item.description: Lawmakers continue to pay tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died suddenly over the weekend. CBS News' Taurean Small and Major Garrett report.

item.title: Darline Graham Nordone, Lindsey Graham's sister, appointed to fill Senate seat
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster announced his pick to fill the vacancy left by Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.

item.title: 7/13: CBS Evening News
item.description: Man shot and killed by ICE agent in Maine was not the subject of a warrant, lawmakers say; Lindsey Graham's sister picked to fill Senate seat.

item.title: What to know as Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline, is appointed to fill his Senate seat
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline Graham Nordone, was announced as his interim replacement on Monday as the state prepares to hold a special election. Graham died unexpectedly over the weekend. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham just died of aorta disease. My husband did too.
item.description: Lindsey Graham's aorta tore at 71. Grant Wahl's burst at 49. One is common and age-driven; the other is inherited, silent, and findable.

item.title: 7/13: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline, is appointed to fill his Senate seat; U.S. launches new strikes against Iran.

item.title: 7/11: CBS Saturday Morning
item.description: Hundreds were rescued from flooding in Missouri. President Trump threatened to "decimate and destroy" Iran if they try to assassinate him. Plus, Barstool Sports' Dave Portnoy sat down with Kelly O'Grady to discuss his new book.
